title: Settings loader, merger, and validator
purpose: Implement the settings loading pipeline: discover settings files at up to three paths, parse JSON, merge layers with field-type-appropriate strategies, and validate the result. This is the core of the configuration system — it turns files on disk into a merged NornSettings that downstream code can consume.
task: Create norn::config::loader with load_settings() that discovers and parses settings files. Create norn::config::merge with merge_settings() implementing five-layer precedence with field-type-specific merge strategies (scalar override, deny-additive, allow-concatenate, hook-extend, MCP-by-name, tool-deep-merge). Create norn::config::validate with validate_settings() that checks duration strings, permission patterns, and MCP server shapes.

requirements.title: Settings file discovery
requirements.spec: load_settings() SHALL discover settings files at three paths: ~/.norn/settings.json (user), .norn/settings.json (project), .norn/settings.local.json (local). Missing files SHALL be treated as empty settings (all-None). The function SHALL accept an explicit working directory for project-level discovery.
acceptance: load_settings(cwd: &Path) defined in norn::config::loader
Returns Result<LoadedSettings, ConfigError> where LoadedSettings holds user, project, and local layers
User path resolved via norn::config::paths::settings_file()
Project path is cwd/.norn/settings.json
Local path is cwd/.norn/settings.local.json
Missing file produces NornSettings::default() for that layer (no error)
Test: missing files produce all-None settings

requirements.title: JSON parsing with typed errors
requirements.spec: WHEN a settings file exists but contains malformed JSON, THE SYSTEM SHALL return a ConfigError naming the file path and the parse error. It SHALL NOT panic or silently ignore the file.
acceptance: Malformed JSON produces ConfigError::InvalidConfig with file path in the reason
serde_json parse error message included in the reason
Test: malformed JSON file produces descriptive error

requirements.title: Five-layer merge for scalar fields
requirements.spec: merge_settings() SHALL merge NornSettings layers in order: compiled defaults, user, project, local, CLI overrides. For scalar fields (Option<T>), higher-precedence non-None value SHALL win.
acceptance: merge_settings defined in norn::config::merge
Accepts user, project, local layers plus a CLI override layer
Returns a single merged NornSettings
Scalar: local Some(x) wins over project Some(y) wins over user Some(z)
Scalar: None at higher layer preserves lower layer's value
Test: three-layer scalar merge with mixed None/Some values

requirements.title: Permission deny-additive merge
requirements.spec: Permission deny arrays SHALL union across all layers. A deny rule at any layer cannot be removed by a higher-precedence layer. Permission allow arrays SHALL concatenate across layers and deduplicate.
acceptance: Deny: user [A, B] + project [C] + local [] produces [A, B, C]
Deny: local cannot remove a user-level deny
Allow: user [X] + project [X, Y] produces [X, Y] (deduplicated)
Ask: same concatenate-deduplicate as allow
Test: deny-additive merge verified
Test: allow-deduplicate merge verified

requirements.title: Hook and MCP merge strategies
requirements.spec: Hook arrays SHALL merge by event type: project hooks extend user hooks within the same event. MCP servers SHALL merge by name: same-name project server overrides user server definition.
acceptance: Hooks: user pre_tool [A] + project pre_tool [B] produces pre_tool [A, B]
Hooks: project does not remove user hooks of the same event type
MCP: user {s1: def_a} + project {s1: def_b, s2: def_c} produces {s1: def_b, s2: def_c}
MCP: same-name project server fully replaces user server (not deep-merged)
Test: hook extend merge verified
Test: MCP by-name merge verified

requirements.title: Tool config deep merge
requirements.spec: Tool configuration SHALL deep-merge: a specific key override at project level preserves sibling keys from user level.
acceptance: User tools.write.max_code_lines=500 + project tools.write.length_overrides=[...] produces both keys present
Project tools.write.max_code_lines=800 overrides user's 500 for that key
Test: deep merge preserves sibling keys across layers

requirements.title: Settings validation
requirements.spec: validate_settings() SHALL validate the merged settings: duration strings via humantime, permission patterns for syntactic well-formedness, MCP server entries for required fields. Unknown top-level keys SHALL emit tracing::warn, not an error.
acceptance: validate_settings defined in norn::config::validate
Invalid duration string produces ConfigError naming the field and value
Invalid permission pattern produces ConfigError naming the pattern
MCP server missing both command and url produces ConfigError
Unknown top-level keys produce tracing::warn (not error)
Valid settings pass without error
Test: invalid duration caught
Test: valid settings pass

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify builder.rs — that is NC-004
SHALL NOT modify the NornSettings struct definition — that was NC-002
SHALL NOT implement hook execution — that is the hooks cluster
SHALL NOT implement MCP server lifecycle — that is a separate concern
SHALL NOT implement permission pattern matching/evaluation at runtime — that is downstream
verification: cargo test -p norn — all tests pass
Merge: three-layer scalar merge produces correct precedence (verified by test)
Merge: deny arrays are union-merged (verified by test)
Merge: hooks extend, not replace (verified by test)
Validation: invalid duration produces descriptive error (verified by test)
Loading: missing file produces empty settings, not error (verified by test)
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
